human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cannabis-processing specificity, source quality, testing and hold discipline, extraction-safety boundary posture, lifecycle-state clarity, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cannabis processing execution, source package lineage, batch records, testing and QA hold handling, transfer readiness, and completion evidence rather than legal interpretation, remediation approval, or final release authority.
Authority sources cover California DCC regulations and manufacturing checklists plus Massachusetts CCC testing and seed-to-sale orders, rather than generic manufacturing references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported release, completion without proof, failed testing and remediation routing, extraction-safety blockers, adjacent-lane rejection, policy conflicts, and multi-jurisdiction tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations/processing-operations-cannabis-specialist/ so cannabis processing remains distinct from adjacent compliance, testing, distribution, inventory, engineering, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported release, false completion attempts, formulation or label overreach, extraction-safety gaps, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated processing operations cannabis specialist for batch execution, testing and hold handling, transfer readiness, and completion evidence capture with explicit cannabis-specific controls.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
